surface.name: Quick start guide: workspace, email channel, test person, first campaign email
surface.selection_basis: The official documentation presents this as the hosted path from account setup to Send your first campaign email. It reaches the requested first-success boundary without extending into post-success production work.

entry_point.developer_intent: A Customer.io campaign sends its message to the test person and the email arrives in the test inbox.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has no prior Customer.io setup for this journey.
The journey starts at account creation and stops at the first documented successful outcome.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: A Customer.io campaign sends its message to the test person and the email arrives in the test inbox.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The test inbox receives the campaign email.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The official documentation leads the developer to: Send your first campaign email.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This is the first observable happy-path result on the selected Quick start guide: workspace, email channel, test person, first campaign email surface; later configuration and production hardening occur after success.

prerequisites.requirement: An email sending channel configured as required by the quick start.
An inbox address that can be used for the test person.

primary_path.action: Create or sign in to Customer.io and create or select a workspace.
primary_path.input: A new developer starting at account creation
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Configure the workspace email channel using the quick-start path.
primary_path.output: The email channel is available for campaign messages.
primary_path.success_signal: The email channel is available for campaign messages.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Add a test person using an email address you can inspect.
primary_path.output: The test person appears in the workspace.
primary_path.success_signal: The test person appears in the workspace.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Create the quick-start campaign and compose its email message.
primary_path.output: The campaign is ready to send to the test person.
primary_path.success_signal: The campaign is ready to send to the test person.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Trigger or start the campaign for the test person.
primary_path.output: Customer.io records the email as sent.
primary_path.success_signal: Customer.io records the email as sent.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Open the test inbox and confirm receipt of the campaign message.
primary_path.output: The first campaign email is visible in the inbox.
primary_path.success_signal: The first campaign email is visible in the inbox.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

friction_gates.description: Email sending configuration must be usable before the campaign can deliver.
Inbox filtering or delivery latency can delay the observable email receipt.

sources.title: Send your first campaign email
evidence_supported: A Customer.io campaign sends its message to the test person and the email arrives in the test inbox.
The test inbox receives the campaign email.

uncertainties.question: Does the current fresh-account UI exactly match every navigation label in the documented Send your first campaign email path?
uncertainties.impact: Navigation wording may differ while the required outcome and boundary remain the same.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official documentation establishes the journey but account-specific UI state can vary.
